title: The cobbler at the end of the street
summary: Describe a shoe-repair shop and the craftsman who runs it.
topic: A cobbler's shop in an old city neighbourhood
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences describing a cobbler's shop and the person who works there. Say where the shop is, what it looks like, and what the cobbler does. Use present simple and adverbs of frequency (always, usually, often, sometimes).
hintStarters: At the end of the street, there is a small shop where…
The cobbler always sits near the window and…
People in the neighbourhood often bring their old shoes because…

hintVocab.meaningVi: thợ đóng giày, thợ sửa giày
mòn, cũ kỹ không dùng được nữa

sampleText: At the end of a narrow street in Istanbul's Balat district, there is a shoe-repair shop smaller than a kitchen. Mehmet, the cobbler, sits there every day from eight in the morning until the light goes. He always works near the small window so he can see what his hands are doing. The shop smells of leather and old polish. A few pairs of shoes hang on nails along the wall, waiting to be collected. Mehmet usually works in silence. He sometimes hums a slow song, but he never looks up when a customer walks in. He knows the sound of the door. He repairs the soles and heels of old shoes with the same steady hands he has used for forty years. Some of his customers are young people who bring expensive trainers. Others are elderly women from the neighbourhood who always bring the same black court shoes. Mehmet fixes them all without much comment. He charges very little. His tools — needles, wax thread, a small hammer — sit in a row on the wooden shelf above his head. He rarely throws anything away. Outside on the street, the city is loud and fast. Inside, Mehmet carries on. He says he will work until his eyes are too tired to see. Nobody in the street imagines a day without his little shop.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: nhỏ hơn cả một căn bếp
cho đến khi hết ánh sáng (trời tối)

sampleAnnotations.phrase: He always works near the small window
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Ông ấy luôn làm việc gần cửa sổ nhỏ

sampleAnnotations.phrase: smells of leather and old polish
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: có mùi da và xi giày cũ

sampleAnnotations.phrase: hang on nails along the wall
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: treo trên những cái đinh dọc theo tường

sampleAnnotations.phrase: He sometimes hums a slow song
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Đôi khi ông ấy khe khẽ hát một bài nhạc chậm

sampleAnnotations.phrase: He knows the sound of the door
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Ông ấy nhận ra tiếng mở cửa
đôi bàn tay vững vàng như mọi khi

sampleAnnotations.phrase: he has used for forty years
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: mà ông ấy đã dùng suốt bốn mươi năm
Ông ấy hiếm khi vứt bỏ thứ gì
Không ai trong khu phố hình dung được

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Adverbs of frequency in present simple
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Trạng từ tần suất (always, usually, sometimes, rarely, never) thường đứng trước động từ chính nhưng sau động từ 'to be'. Ví dụ trong bài: 'He always works near the small window', 'He sometimes hums', 'He rarely throws anything away'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Adverbs of frequency (always, usually, sometimes, rarely, never) go before the main verb but after 'to be'. Examples from the sample: 'He always works near the small window', 'He sometimes hums', 'He rarely throws anything away'.

sampleTranslationVi: Cuối một con phố hẹp ở khu Balat, Istanbul, có một tiệm sửa giày nhỏ hơn cả một căn bếp. Mehmet, người thợ đóng giày, ngồi ở đó mỗi ngày từ tám giờ sáng cho đến khi trời tối. Ông ấy luôn làm việc gần cửa sổ nhỏ để có thể nhìn thấy đôi tay mình đang làm gì. Tiệm có mùi da thuộc và xi giày cũ. Vài đôi giày treo trên những cái đinh dọc theo tường, chờ được lấy về. Mehmet thường làm việc trong im lặng. Đôi khi ông khe khẽ hát một bài nhạc chậm, nhưng ông không bao giờ nhìn lên khi có khách bước vào. Ông nhận ra tiếng mở cửa. Ông sửa đế và gót của những đôi giày cũ bằng đôi bàn tay vững vàng mà ông đã dùng suốt bốn mươi năm. Một số khách hàng là người trẻ mang đến giày thể thao đắt tiền. Những người khác là các bà cụ trong khu phố luôn mang đến cùng một đôi giày đen. Mehmet sửa tất cả mà không nói nhiều. Ông tính phí rất thấp. Dụng cụ của ông — kim, chỉ sáp, một cái búa nhỏ — xếp hàng trên kệ gỗ phía trên đầu. Ông hiếm khi vứt bỏ thứ gì. Ngoài phố, thành phố ồn ào và vội vã. Bên trong, Mehmet vẫn tiếp tục làm việc. Ông nói ông sẽ làm đến khi mắt quá mỏi để nhìn. Không ai trong khu phố hình dung được một ngày thiếu đi tiệm nhỏ của ông.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural sentence describing where the shop is.
options: At the end of a narrow street, there is a small shoe-repair shop.
At the end of a narrow street, there has a small shoe-repair shop.
At the end of a narrow street, there are a small shoe-repair shop.
At the end of a narrow street, it is a small shoe-repair shop.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'There is' is the correct existential structure for introducing a singular location or object.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the sentence that correctly uses an adverb of frequency.
options: He works always near the window.
He always works near the window.
Always he works near the window.
He works near always the window.
mcQuestions.explanation: Adverbs of frequency like 'always' go before the main verb in a statement.
mcQuestions.hint: Where does 'always' sit relative to the main verb?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which word means the bottom part of a shoe that touches the ground?
options: heel
sole
toe
lace
mcQuestions.explanation: The 'sole' is the flat bottom of a shoe. The 'heel' is only the back raised part.
mcQuestions.hint: Which part covers the whole underside of the shoe?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the sentence that correctly uses 'smells of'.
options: The shop smells of leather and polish.
The shop smells like of leather and polish.
The shop smells leather and polish.
The shop is smelling of leather and polish.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Smells of' is the fixed collocation for describing a persistent smell. 'Smells like' needs no 'of'.
mcQuestions.hint: Which preposition follows 'smells' when describing what a place smells like?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best describes a craftsman working quietly without interruption?
options: He usually works in silence.
He usually works in quietly.
He usually works in the silent.
He usually works with silence.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Works in silence' is the correct fixed phrase. 'Quietly' is an adverb, not a noun, so 'in quietly' is wrong.
mcQuestions.hint: Which preposition and noun form the correct phrase for working quietly?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct sentence about how long Mehmet has used his hands.
options: He used his steady hands for forty years.
He has use his steady hands for forty years.
He has used his steady hands for forty years.
He use his steady hands since forty years.
mcQuestions.explanation: For an action starting in the past and continuing now, use present perfect: 'has used' + 'for + period'.
mcQuestions.hint: Which tense + 'for' shows something that started in the past and is still true?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the sentence where 'rarely' is placed correctly.
options: He throws rarely anything away.
He rarely throws anything away.
Rarely he throws anything away.
He throws anything rarely away.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Rarely' is a negative-leaning adverb of frequency and goes before the main verb.
mcQuestions.hint: Adverbs of frequency sit before the main verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase means 'shoes that are too old or damaged to use'?
options: well-made shoes
worn-out shoes
polished shoes
steady shoes
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Worn out' means damaged or too old through long use. It describes something that can no longer be used properly.
mcQuestions.hint: Which phrase describes something damaged from too much use?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the most natural sentence describing items hanging on the wall.
options: A few pairs of shoes hang on nails along the wall.
A few pairs of shoes hangs on nails along the wall.
A few pair of shoes hang on nails along the wall.
A few pairs of shoes are hang on nails along the wall.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'A few pairs' is plural, so 'hang' (not 'hangs') is correct. 'Are hang' is wrong; the simple present 'hang' is enough.
mcQuestions.hint: Check subject-verb agreement: 'a few pairs' is plural.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the sentence that correctly uses 'carry on'.
options: He carries on to work every day.
He carries on working every day.
He carries on for work every day.
He carries on at working every day.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Carry on' is followed by a gerund (-ing form): 'carry on working', 'carry on doing'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes a craftsman who does not chat with customers?
options: He fixes the shoes with much comment.
He fixes the shoes without much comment.
He fixes the shoes without many comment.
He fixes the shoes with no any comment.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Without much comment' is the correct fixed phrase. 'Comment' is uncountable here, so 'much' is correct, not 'many'.
mcQuestions.hint: Is 'comment' countable or uncountable in this phrase?

criticalThinkingQuestion: Mehmet charges very little for his repairs. Do you think a craftsman like him can survive in a modern city? What would be lost if his shop closed?
